semanticCore: Suppressing anger, choking, silence, locking gate, or throat-block
quranicRelevance: Suppressing anger (Kazimeen - 'wal-kathimeena al-ghaytha'), choked with silent grief (Kazeem - 'wahuwa katheem'), and Jonah choked/distressed ('wahuwa makthoom').

keyDerivations.meaning: To suppress, choke back, lock up, keep silent, or swallow anger
Choked with silent grief, suppressed, sorrowful, or melancholy
Suppressed, choked, distressed, or confined in sorrow

figurativeOrRareUsages: الْكَاظِمِينَ الْغَيْظَ (3:134): Those who suppress/swallow their anger (high moral self-control).
إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ (68:48): When he (Jonah inside the whale) called out while he was choked/distressed.
discardedNoise: Modern water bottles or thermal flasks (Katham) - derived modern usage.
Obscure details of camel throat valves.
summaryEn: The root represents swallowing/choking, contrasting the social virtue of swallowing anger (Kadhimeen) with the physical choking of Jonah inside the whale.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر كتمان الغيظ وخناق الحزن، مادحاً كظم الغضب في المعاملات الاجتماعية، ومصوّراً خناق يونس في بطن الحوت (مكظوم).
rawText: كَظَائِمُ [pl. of كِظَامَةٌ] Subterranean conduits for water. (TA in art. قنو.) كَظَمَ غَيْظَهُ 1 He repressed, or restrained, his wrath, or rage. (K.) === كَظَمَ, aor. ﹻ, inf. n. كَظْمٌ, He restrained himself. (TA.) === مَا يَكْظِمُ عَلَى جِرَّةٍ; and لَا يَكْظِمٌ عَلَى جِرَّتِهِ: see جِرَّةٌ. === كَظَمَ عَلَى غَيْظِهِ i. q. كَظَمَ غَيْظَهُ. (TA.)
